meta.note: Refactored API structure - Actor-based mutations, Resource-based reads

product_overview: The Provider Event Management API enables providers to manage their own events (availability slots). This API uses a hybrid architecture: mutations (POST, PUT, DELETE) are organized by actor (/api/provider/*) while read operations (GET) remain resource-based (/api/events, /api/providers). Providers can create, update, and delete their own events within business hours, subject to validation rules.
architecture_note: This API follows a hybrid approach: Actor-based mutations (/api/provider/*) for clear authorization, Resource-based reads (/api/events, /api/providers) for clear resource access. Mutations require PROVIDER role and verify ownership.
core_goals: Enable providers to create, update, and delete their own events (availability slots).
Ensure providers can only manage events that belong to them.
Enforce business rules (time windows, no past events, no modifications to booked events).
Provide read access to events and provider information for all authenticated users.
Maintain clear separation between actor-based mutations and resource-based reads.
key_features: Event Creation API to create availability slots with validation (time windows, no past dates).
Event Update API to modify event details (title, description) for unbooked events only.
Event Deletion API to remove unbooked events.
Event List API (resource-based) to retrieve events with optional filtering.
Event Detail API (resource-based) to get specific event information.
Provider List API (resource-based) to retrieve provider information.
Secure endpoints protected by Better Auth session authentication and provider role authorization.
Ownership verification ensuring providers can only manage their own events.
user_flow_summary: Provider logs in and authenticates using Better Auth session cookies.
Provider retrieves their provider record from /api/providers?userId=<userId>.
Provider creates availability slots via POST /api/provider/events with validation.
Provider views their events via GET /api/events?providerId=<providerId>.
Provider updates event details (title, description) via PUT /api/provider/events/:id for unbooked events.
Provider deletes unbooked events via DELETE /api/provider/events/:id.
System validates time windows, prevents past events, and blocks modifications to booked events.
validation_criteria: All API endpoints enforce authentication and return appropriate 401/403 responses for unauthorized access.
Provider mutation endpoints verify that the provider owns the event being modified.
Event creation validates time windows (default: 8:00-20:00 in configured timezone).
Event creation prevents creating events in the past.
Event update/delete prevents modifications to booked events.
Provider can only create events for their own providerId (verified via requireProviderHook and ownership check).
Read endpoints (GET /api/events, GET /api/providers) work for all authenticated users.

authentication.note: Use session cookies, NOT bearer tokens. Authenticate via POST /api/auth/sign-in/email first.

test_scenarios: Create event with valid data (within business hours, future date)
Create event validation (missing required fields, past dates, outside business hours)
Update event with valid data (unbooked event, owned by provider)
Update event validation (booked event, not owned by provider, missing title)
Delete event with valid data (unbooked event, owned by provider)
Delete event validation (booked event, not owned by provider)
Authorization checks (provider can only manage own events)

actor_based_mutations.note: These endpoints are actor-based (/api/provider/*) - clearly indicate who can perform the action

endpoints.description: Creates a new event (availability slot) for the authenticated provider. Validates time windows, prevents past dates, verifies provider ownership, and calculates duration automatically.

authorization.ownership_check: providerId must belong to authenticated user

providerId.description: ID of the provider creating the event. Must match the authenticated user's provider ID.

start.description: Event start time (must be in the future and within business hours)

end.description: Event end time (must be within business hours and after start time)

validation_rules: providerId, title, start, and end are required
start date must be in the future
start time must be between AVAILABILITY_START_HOUR (default: 8:00) and AVAILABILITY_END_HOUR (default: 20:00) in configured timezone
end time must not exceed AVAILABILITY_END_HOUR
providerId must belong to authenticated user (verified via requireProviderHook and ownership check)

scenarios: Missing required fields (providerId, title, start, or end)
Start date is in the past
Event time is outside business hours (before 8:00 or after 20:00)
providerId does not belong to authenticated user

body.error: Forbidden - Only the provider can create their events

endpoints.description: Updates an event's title and/or description. Only allows updates to unbooked events that belong to the authenticated provider.

authorization.ownership_check: Event must belong to authenticated provider

validation_rules: Event must belong to authenticated provider
Event must not be booked (isBooked = false)

scenarios: Event is booked (cannot update booked events)
Event does not belong to authenticated provider

body.error: Forbidden - Only the provider can update their events

endpoints.description: Deletes an event. Only allows deletion of unbooked events that belong to the authenticated provider.

authorization.ownership_check: Event must belong to authenticated provider

validation_rules: Event must belong to authenticated provider
Event must not be booked (isBooked = false)

scenarios: Event is booked (cannot delete booked events)
Event does not belong to authenticated provider

body.error: Forbidden - Only the provider can delete their events

resource_based_reads.note: These endpoints are resource-based (/api/events, /api/providers) - clearly indicate what resource is being accessed

endpoints.description: Retrieves a list of events with optional filtering. Available to all authenticated users. Used by providers to view their events and by clients to browse available events.

available.description: Filter to only unbooked, future events

endpoints.description: Retrieves a specific event by ID. Available to all authenticated users.
Retrieves a list of providers with optional filtering. Available to all authenticated users. Used by providers to find their own provider record.

userId.description: Filter providers by user ID (used by providers to find their own record)

endpoints.description: Retrieves a specific provider by ID. Available to all authenticated users.

authentication.description: This application uses Better Auth with session-based authentication (cookies), NOT bearer tokens. Authentication is done via POST request to /api/auth/sign-in/email with email and password. The response includes session cookies in Set-Cookie headers. Use requests.Session() to automatically manage cookies. Include session cookies in all subsequent requests. DO NOT use Authorization: Bearer headers.

credentials.name: Jones Doe
credentials.note: This user has PROVIDER role and can test all provider mutation endpoints. GET endpoints work with any authenticated user.

AVAILABILITY_START_HOUR.description: Earliest hour for event availability (24-hour format)

AVAILABILITY_END_HOUR.description: Latest hour for event availability (24-hour format)

AVAILABILITY_TIME_ZONE.description: Time zone for availability window validation

requirements: Use session-based authentication (Better Auth cookies), NOT bearer tokens
Test resource reads at /api/events and /api/providers endpoints (resource-based)
Verify ownership checks - providers can only manage their own events
Test validation rules (time windows, past dates, booked events)
Test authorization - unauthorized users cannot access provider mutation endpoints
Use the provided provider credentials for mutation tests
Test GET endpoints work with any authenticated user
